paperTitle: Active Sequential Posterior Estimation for Sample-Efficient Simulation-Based Inference

method.name: Active Sequential Posterior Estimation
method.description: An active learning scheme formulated to improve sample efficiency in simulation-based inference.
keyFeatures: Selects samples expected to target epistemic uncertainty in the underlying probabilistic model.
Makes acquisition evaluation simple and efficient when using any Bayesian Neural Density Estimator (NDE).

problemStatement.coreChallenge: Inverse problems are difficult to solve using black-box simulators.
specificIssues: Simulators are often computationally expensive to evaluate.
The implicit likelihood function is generally intractable.
The dimensionality of high-fidelity outputs is typically prohibitive.

technologies: Normalizing Flows (e.g., Real NVP, MAE, MAF)

limitationsOfPriorWork.targetMethod: Sequential Neural Posterior Estimation (SNPE) and similar methods
issues: Updated proposal distributions do not account for the current parameters of the NDE.
Parameter samples in a batch are drawn independently, failing to utilize batch optimization.
This leads to simulation runs with high expected information overlap and wasted computation.

title: Active Sequential Posterior Estimation for Sample-Efficient Simulation-Based Inference
